| [](https://substack.com/redirect/e318869f-e341-4a69-b4c7-44275df84786?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Washington double-crossing the Delaware
The phrase "Second American Revolution" has been in popular currency almost since the original American Revolution ended. Thomas Jefferson [believed his victory in the election of 1800 comprised a Second American Revolution](https://substack.com/redirect/182c98de-cef5-4b41-9547-a737e1885ec0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). High school history books categorize [the War of 1812 as the Second American Revolution](https://substack.com/redirect/50b8f2d7-ae10-4d2b-9292-c31272dc09aa?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). Eminent historians [Charles and Mary Beard, Eric Foner, and Bruce Levine, ](https://substack.com/redirect/196e002c-0b1e-4a77-a8f9-c4e46a29b17f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)to name a few, have argued that the Civil War and the subsequent Reconstruction period was a Second American Revolution. A plausible case could also be made that the Andrew Jackson presidency, either of the Roosevelt presidencies, or the civil rights movement of the 1960s was a Second American Revolution.
Not so. In _States and Social Revolutions_ , the Harvard political scientist Theda Skocpol defines social revolutions as "rapid, basic transformations of a society's state and class structures," distinguished from other kinds of revolution "by the combination of two coincidences: the coincidence of societal structural change with class upheaval; and the coincidence of political with social transformation," whereas revolutions that are merely political "transform state structures but not social structures...." None of those proposed Second American Revolutions, not even the Civil War, transformed the U.S. state structure--that is, the _architecture of our government_. _That_ hasn't happened since the ratification of the Constitution in 1789.
On the contrary, the U.S. state structure has proved remarkably durable. Since the drafting of the Constitution, there have been momentous social revolutions in France, in Russia, and in China--but no dictionary-definition revolutions, either social or political, in the United States. For the last 236 years, Americans have reliably enjoyed the same three branches of government; the same viable if flawed system of checks and balances; the same free and fair elections that became increasingly more fair and free as the decades went on. To use a metaphor: we've put significant additions on the old Colonial, we've made extensive renovations to the layout, but the original structure still stands, built on its bedrock foundation of the separation of powers.
For all the righteous blather about fealty to the Constitution, the Make America Great Again movement has made its prime objective the annihilation of the existing government. In the Trump Redux, our metaphorical old house is slated for demolition. The dwelling is being ripped to the studs, and the studs fed into Elon Musk's "woodchipper." What did we think the logical conclusion would be of their much-ballyhooed plan to dismantle the administrative state?
This is not an overhaul. This is an overthrow.
The Old Guard had four full years to root out the traitors and failed to do so. As I wrote in the introduction to _The Age of Unreality_ , the Biden presidency, which we hoped would decisively win the battle for the soul of America, proved merely to be a four-year hiatus, a Delaware speed-trap on the I-95 to tyranny, as insufficient as it was brief. Merrick Garland preserved his precious "norms" at the expense of the American experiment. Before the election, John Roberts and the Leonard Leo Court granted the President near-kingly powers--which Biden never considered using but Trump will merrily exploit. And here we are.
In the long annals of the United States, we have elected good presidents and great ones, mediocrities and failures. Some, like Jackson, Lincoln, and FDR, tested the limits of presidential power. But none of the 44 previous Chief Executives--not Warren G. Harding, not Andrew Johnson, not even Richard Nixon--was as nakedly corrupt as the current occupant of the White House, or as overtly contemptuous of democracy. The vote should not have been close, but we sent him back to Washington anyway. We chose [Barabbas](https://substack.com/redirect/b60c96a5-afc1-4eff-919b-cbcff3cbc56c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
What failed on January 6, 2021 triumphed on January 20, 2025. It was all part of the same sustained, coordinated assault on our democratic form of government. After the initial setback, the MAGA armies have been steadily advancing. And now the house that is our structure of state is being razed.
The Second American Revolution is drawing to its close. Democracy is on life support. The nation is under fascist occupation. And most of the country hasn't the foggiest notion that there's even a war on.
**Blue Amp 's a simple idea: Insiders turned fearless outsiders **_**telling you what others won 't**_**. We 're un-bought; We Need Your Support.**_**Please become a paid subscriber**_
[ Upgrade to paid](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuYmx1ZWFtcC5jby9zdWJzY3JpYmU_.8PbVZD-1YM1oj8S3WYz4bar7_740HVHRGNveHesGLOc?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget-preamble&utm_content=161408691)
* * *
The narrative of Trump ushering in another American Revolution is not my invention. The MAGA thought leaders have been invoking Revolutionary War language and peacocking in Revolutionary War imagery for a solid decade now. The blown-up posters of the Constitution and the Declaration of Independence, the "Don't Tread On Me" flags, the countless references to the Boston Tea Party, even the monomaniacal focus on tax reform--all of that is deliberate and of a piece. Rep. Lauren Boebert's notorious tweet on the morning of J6 is an example of this performative gesture:
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/d6d19195-4bed-4ea4-8a32-b0aa1e2466e1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Steve Bannon's podcast is called _The War Room_ for a reason. "I'm not a journalist. I'm not in the media. This is a military headquarters for a populist revolt," [the felon told David Brooks of the ](https://substack.com/redirect/ba5e43d7-96c3-4d86-8120-9861c4b104f7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_[New York Times](https://substack.com/redirect/ba5e43d7-96c3-4d86-8120-9861c4b104f7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_[ back in July](https://substack.com/redirect/ba5e43d7-96c3-4d86-8120-9861c4b104f7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). "You're a conservative, but you're not dangerous. You're reasonable. We're not reasonable. We're unreasonable because we're fighting for a republic. And we're never going to be reasonable until we get what we [want]. We're not looking to compromise. We're looking to win....[O]n the fundamental direction of the country, we are separate. We are two different worldviews. And those worldviews can't be bridged."
The republic he's fighting for is a republic in the same way that a Soviet Socialist Republic was a republic--populist or not, he's no small-d democrat--but Bannon is right about the irreconcilable differences of the two factions. This is a binary choice. The country can't very well be a democracy _and_ a dictatorship.
Disgraced former national security adviser Mike Flynn has long couched his rightwing nationalist inklings in Revolutionary War rhetoric--part of an extensive psy-op to frame corruption, criminality, and treason as some sort of patriotic enterprise. Before the election, we were in a _third_ American Revolution:
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/671f652c-85a5-4ec9-bc45-c8bcd586389c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
After the inauguration, Flynn discounted the Civil War and got down to two. Note his casual use of a Benjamin Franklin quote to dress sedition in Revolutionary War garb:
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/637322d9-807a-4f77-be5b-c410877e8dbe?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
And you won't be surprised to learn that the Apartheid Nazi billionaire tasked with dismantling the so-called Administrative State--and plundering the nation's resources (money, data, technology, land, water, etc.) in the process, to create a Russia-in-the-90s-style oligarchy--has also taken to wrapping himself in the flag. (Also note: he stole the idea from someone else, as is his wont.)
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/49aad59a-599a-43da-86fd-bdae00f2fe64?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/4e2c3db8-ac90-4280-b997-0bd141964bf0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
To the true believers, Donald isn't the leader of the movement as much as the movement's secret weapon--an instrument by which the presidency becomes a throne, and our nation's power and wealth are redistributed to the MAGA oligarchs.
"Trump is a revolution," Bannon tells Brooks. "Remember, General Washington, the revolution and the foundation, and then Lincoln, the birth of the new America....I hate to say this, but Trump's the third. Trump is taking America back to its more constitutional Republic for the third time...."
* * *
Google "Second American Revolution" and many of the responses ping back to remarks that Kevin Roberts--["Cowboy Catholic,"](https://substack.com/redirect/8c0118b6-ff3b-4bfe-b3cb-0a689a4f86a3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) head of the Heritage Foundation, and prime mover of Project 2025--[made on Bannon's podcast two days before Independence Day, 2024](https://substack.com/redirect/b53dae6c-0396-4f88-8247-97da8d81b23e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
"In spite of all this nonsense from the Left, we are going to win," he assured the _War Room_ listeners. "We're in the process of taking this country back. No one in the audience should be despairing....[W]e are in the process of the Second American Revolution, which will remain bloodless if the Left allows it to be."
Whoa, Nelly! **A Second American Revolution, which will remain bloodless** _**if the Left allows it to be**_**.** That was a provocative thing for anyone to say, much less the head of the Heritage Foundation, an august conservative think tank founded over half a century ago; its executive director is not some fringe podcaster, out Nazi, or QAnon freak. In that interview, Roberts confirmed that _he and his comrades were openly attempting to overthrow the government_(that is, after all, what "revolution" means), and he doubled down with an implicit threat of mass violence. That the threat was veiled made it no less ominous.
Distilling the theory Ted Gurr lays out in _Why Men Rebel_ , Skocpol writes: "Political violence occurs when many people in society become angry, especially if existing cultural and practical conditions provide encouragement for aggression against political targets." Well, tens of millions of Americans are fucking _furious_ right now--all across the political spectrum. Trump has spent years subtly encouraging political violence; the point of his calling out specific judges by name, for example, is to foment stochastic terrorism. Other MAGA leaders, like Mike Flynn's flying monkey and self-styled Secretary of Retribution Ivan Raiklin, have been more explicit. On the other side of the horseshoe, plenty of progressives have lionized Luigi Mangione. As for "practical conditions," I mean, this is a nation armed to the teeth; our citizenry is better armed than most armies. Given all the powder in the keg, an uptick in political violence seems inevitable--just a matter of time. Any political rhetoric that fans the flames could make the whole thing explode. By talking like that, Roberts was openly flirting with disaster.
Even the bumbling legacy media seemed to appreciate that Roberts had crossed some sort of dangerous line. But it ended there. For the most part, the press failed to connect the dots between the Heritage Foundation, the other sponsors of Project 2025, Leonard Leo's dark-money operations, the Dark Enlightenment thought leaders (including the now-VP JD Vance), the libertarian arm of Silicon Valley, Flynn and his acolytes, the Bannon contingent, the Christian Nationalists, and various and sundry other groups united in common cause against "the Cathedral," "the Regime," "the Administrative State," and, ultimately, democracy. Trump's disavowal of Project 2025 on the campaign trail--an obvious lie even in the moment--was enough to get the legacy media hacks off the scent. But Roberts, to his credit, has always been perfectly transparent about the movement's intentions. I mean, they _literally_ wrote a book about what they were planning to do!
Two weeks ago, Roberts, [writing in ](https://substack.com/redirect/ce4a0009-27ac-4c6d-8652-f3e69e50baa6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_[The Spectator](https://substack.com/redirect/ce4a0009-27ac-4c6d-8652-f3e69e50baa6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_ , gave a sort of progress report of the Second American Revolution. After opening with a reference to Patrick Henry's "Liberty or Death" speech--"one of my personal favorites"--he writes:
> > The good news is that we are well on our way. Right now, President Trump's executive orders and decisive actions are systematically dismantling the deep state's hard power. On his first day in office, he terminated all remote work arrangements for federal employees. Then, he introduced an unprecedented, deferred resignation program which convinced more than 75,000 unelected bureaucrats to resign their posts. And last month he signed a new executive order requiring all federal agencies to fire at least four people for every one person they hire. Meanwhile, the Department of Government Efficiency's (DoGE) incredible work has undermined the regime's soft power by exposing its waste and corruption unlike ever before.
>> Make no mistake, these have only been the opening skirmishes in what will be a long fight. Though President Trump's has already delivered a major blow, the deep state remains powerful. Slowly but surely, it is forming its counteroffensive. Corrupt federal judges are blocking the President's executive orders. Colleges and universities are disguising their DEI programs to prevent losing federal funding. Countless bureaucrats are laying low in federal agencies, waiting for the opportunity to upend the people's agenda. Even as fewer and fewer people are tuning in, the mainstream media is still doing everything it can to disparage Trump....
>> We are living in the Second American Revolution. We are stronger than we've ever been, and we must use every moral lever of power at our disposal to restore our founding principles, recover the basic norms of Western civilization (such as the notion that men and women are different), and revive the traditional institutions of American life by restoring their original purpose.
>> Despite the left's fearmongering, none of this requires violence. But it does require us to remember the promise of Patrick Henry that "millions of people, armed in the holy cause of liberty, and in such a country as that which we possess, are invincible by any force which our enemy can send against us."
Roberts's characterization of the Trump/Musk actions as anything other than corrupt, oligarchical, petty, cruel, arbitrary, and profoundly antidemocratic--and therefore unequivocally unAmerican--is either irrefutable evidence of severe mental illness or willful and weaponized mendacity. In his writing, he is fond of inserting allusions to conservative heroes like Ronald Reagan (as in [his foreword to Project 2025's ](https://substack.com/redirect/512e6da7-9879-4b45-ad13-a642a8ab8e8f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_[Mandate for Leadership](https://substack.com/redirect/512e6da7-9879-4b45-ad13-a642a8ab8e8f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_) or, here, Patrick Henry, and then deliberately taking the quotes out of context to make it seem like these liberty-loving legends would somehow sign on to his destruction of our democracy.
The irony is that the Second American Revolution seeks to resurrect the tyranny the first one stamped out. Seventeen seventy-six was _Star Wars_ ; 2025 is _The Empire Strikes Back_. We need Luke to finish his training, Han Solo to thaw, and the Ewoks to get involved right quick, or we're looking at a bleak, regressive, Darth Vader future.
**Blue Amp 's a simple idea: Insiders turned fearless outsiders **_**telling you what others won 't**_**. We 're un-bought; We Need Your Support.**_**Please become a paid subscriber**_
[ Upgrade to paid](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuYmx1ZWFtcC5jby9zdWJzY3JpYmU_.8PbVZD-1YM1oj8S3WYz4bar7_740HVHRGNveHesGLOc?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget-preamble&utm_content=161408691)
* * *
I bring all this up not to be defeatist but to properly frame the struggle. We--that is: the media, Congress, Wall Street, and the American people generally--are in denial about the grim reality of the situation. Before we can possibly build a viable resistance movement, we have to face five key facts:
### **1\. The Second American Revolution is a war.**
"We are stronger than we've ever been, and we must use every moral lever of power at our disposal," Roberts [wrote two weeks ago](https://substack.com/redirect/ce4a0009-27ac-4c6d-8652-f3e69e50baa6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), before assuring us that "none of this requires violence." That's a callback to his "bloodless" comment back in July. The implication is that the MAGA side is perfectly willing to take up arms and slaughter the rest of us if need be. Is there any reason to believe otherwise? We need to stop pretending that this is all a friendly disagreement between two democratic parties. It's not. Bannon is absolutely right when he says, "We are two different worldviews. And those worldviews can't be bridged." (I think a lot of Democrats realize this--it animated the seemingly disproportionate anger at the useless fool Chuck Schumer, who is hopelessly stuck in a past that no longer exists.)
* * *
### **2\. Every day Trump stays in power it becomes harder to dislodge him.**
Even if the House impeached him (which it won't as long as his compromised bootlicker Mike Johnson remains the Speaker) and the Senate convicted him (which it very well might if a vote was cast tomorrow), do we really think he'd voluntarily leave? Who would remove him from the premises? The Praetorian Guard that is the Secret Service? The DOGE goon squad the U.S Marshals have become? The Senate Sergeant at Arms? Pete Fucking Hegseth? Good luck with that.
* * *
### **3\. There is no viable constitutional remedy to correct course.**
To re-establish a functional democracy and reclaim our standing as the leaders of the free world, the _entire MAGA cancer_ must be excised from the political body: Trump, Vance, Speaker Johnson, the entire Cabinet, every last one of his appointees and hires. All the demons, cast out. There is no template for how that would work; Madison and Jefferson didn't game-plan for wholesale treason. (Is there a way to compel all of the traitors to resign en masse? Maybe trading resignation for immunity?) By whatever method, they must _all_ go, and then we must hold new elections as soon as possible--but what happens until President Ocasio-Cortez is sworn in? What does an interim government look like? Under what authority does it exist?
* * *
### **4\. Some of the damage is irreversible.**
Trillions of dollars in wealth have evaporated because of Trump's rock-dumb tariff obsession. Decades of careful humanitarian work went up in smoke when DOGE froze payments to USAID and then blew it up. Russia has been given a lifeline, more time to rally its troops, and fewer defenses to worry about in Ukraine. Bibi is running amok in Gaza. Scientific research hit a brick wall. A new pandemic would potentially wipe a lot of us out because of RFK cuts. Hundreds of thousands if not millions of American lives have been disrupted. Europe doesn't trust us anymore. And Canada now hates us so much that _t[hey have turned on Wayne Gretzky](https://substack.com/redirect/64e745d8-c4bb-45f4-ae78-bd16dde3ee6e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_[ for going MAGA](https://substack.com/redirect/64e745d8-c4bb-45f4-ae78-bd16dde3ee6e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). These are just a few examples; someone could write a long book about how much damage he and Musk have already done--and they're just getting started. This cacophony is merely the overture to a tedious, artless, interminable opera of stupid.
* * *
### **5\. The country cannot survive four years of this.**
We're a mere 78 days into the second Trump Administration. Assuming the Redux is a "normal" four-year term of 1461 days--and, spoiler alert, if Trump is still alive and in office in 2028, he is absolutely going to run for a third term, Twenty Second Amendment be damned--95 percent of his second term is still to come. What fresh horrors await us? Hyperinflation? Martial law imposed on April 20? Roundup of dissidents and free transport to El Salvador? A deadlier pandemic? Nuclear war? It's like heading into the finale of _White Lotus_ : anything is possible, but the only thing we know for sure is that people we love are going to die.
* * *
It is encouraging that Kevin Roberts says we are "living in the Second American Revolution" rather than gloating about his side's total victory. But while Revolution No. 2 may not be complete, it has already succeeded in its first task: destroying the _Ancien R egime Americain_ (incidentally, one of the things the Unabomber goes on about in his manifesto that all these Dark Enlightenment dorks obsess over). We must take the "L" and waste no further time crying over the spilled milk of our former hegemony. To win--to have any hope of winning--we must move forward.
The silver lining here is that if MAGA can be thwarted--if we can, in their parlance, _take back America_ --that would give us the opportunity to repair the Constitutional fault lines that work against sustained democracy. On paper, this ain't hard: Get rid of the Electoral College. Figure out a way to make the Senate more representative of the nation as a whole (i.e., Wyoming should not have the same number of Senators as California).[1](https://substack.com/redirect/5620e09b-f2a9-431c-bf9e-664ca7859fac?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) Expand the Supreme Court and/or impose term limits. Kill _Citizens United_ like it's a puppy and we're Kristi Noem. Make D.C. and Puerto Rico states. That won't put together what Trump has permanently broken, but it would strengthen our democracy going forward.
And here's a final irony. After ten years of Trump repeating what is, to him, a meaningless catchphrase, it will be left to us, the real patriots, the true custodians of this democracy, to make America great again.
Let the Counterrevolution begin!
[1](https://substack.com/redirect/8cab589a-50bb-4333-8204-99b8d3021498?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) Maybe a tiered system, where top 10 in population get 3 senators, bottom 10 get 1?
* * *
Greg Olear is the _L.A. Times_ -bestselling author of three novels and four nonfiction books, including _[Rough Beast: Who Donald Trump Really Is , What He'll Do If Re-Elected, and Why Democracy Must Prevail](https://substack.com/redirect/ebfb1820-aad2-43ed-8648-f8a7307f44fa?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) _(2024). His newest book is _[The Age of Unreality: Essays on Literature& Tyranny](https://substack.com/redirect/3381d91a-9b56-4288-a2ee-66e23fc3c662?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_. He cohosts the weekly live show _[The Five 8](https://substack.com/redirect/aa9b21b1-d4f4-4fa4-abdd-e769c916de7f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_ and hosts the PREVAIL podcast. His Substack column, [PREVAIL](https://substack.com/redirect/16f90478-f0d8-4b35-8557-722421594d28?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), has run since 2019.

clean_body: Trump, Musk, Bannon, Flynn, and Project 2025's Kevin Roberts are actively transforming the structure of our government. When, and how, will we fight back? | | ---|---|--- | | | Forwarded this email? ? for more ? Trump, Musk, Bannon, Flynn, and Project 2025's Kevin Roberts are actively transforming the structure of our government. When, and how, will we fight back? | | | Apr 15| | | ∙| | Guest post ---|---|--- | | | | | | | | | | | | | | ---|---|--- Washington double-crossing the Delaware The phrase "Second American Revolution" has been in popular currency almost since the original American Revolution ended. Thomas Jefferson . High school history books categorize . Eminent historians name a few, have argued that the Civil War and the subsequent Reconstruction period was a Second American Revolution. A plausible case could also be made that the Andrew Jackson presidency, either of the Roosevelt presidencies, or the civil rights movement of the 1960s was a Second American Revolution. Not so. In States and Social Revolutions , the Harvard political scientist Theda Skocpol defines social revolutions as "rapid, basic transformations of a society's state and class structures," distinguished from other kinds of revolution "by the combination of two coincidences: the coincidence of societal structural change with class upheaval; and the coincidence of political with social transformation," whereas revolutions that are merely political "transform state structures but not social structures...." None of those proposed Second American Revolutions, not even the Civil War, transformed the U.S. state structure--that is, the architecture of our government. That hasn't happened since the ratification of the Constitution in 1789. On the contrary, the U.S. state structure has proved remarkably durable. Since the drafting of the Constitution, there have been momentous social revolutions in France, in Russia, and in China--but no dictionary-definition revolutions, either social or political, in the United States. For the last 236 years, Americans have reliably enjoyed the same three branches of government; the same viable if flawed system of checks and balances; the same free and fair elections that became increasingly more fair and free as the decades went on. To use a metaphor: we've put significant additions on the old Colonial, we've made extensive renovations to the layout, but the original structure still stands, built on its bedrock foundation of the separation of powers. For all the righteous blather about fealty to the Constitution, the Make America Great Again movement has made its prime objective the annihilation of the existing government. In the Trump Redux, our metaphorical old house is slated for demolition. The dwelling is being ripped to the studs, and the studs fed into Elon Musk's "woodchipper." What did we think the logical conclusion would be of their much-ballyhooed plan to dismantle the administrative state? This is not an overhaul. This is an overthrow. The Old Guard had four full years to root out the traitors and failed to do so. As I wrote in the introduction to The Age of Unreality , the Biden presidency, which we hoped would decisively win the battle for the soul of America, proved merely to be a four-year hiatus, a Delaware speed-trap on the I-95 to tyranny, as insufficient as it was brief. Merrick Garland preserved his precious "norms" at the expense of the American experiment. Before the election, John Roberts and the Leonard Leo Court granted the President near-kingly powers--which Biden never considered using but Trump will merrily exploit. And here we are. In the long annals of the United States, we have elected good presidents and great ones, mediocrities and failures. Some, like Jackson, Lincoln, and FDR, tested the limits of presidential power. But none of the 44 previous Chief Executives--not Warren G. Harding, not Andrew Johnson, not even Richard Nixon--was as nakedly corrupt as the current occupant of the White House, or as overtly contemptuous of democracy. The vote should not have been close, but we sent him back to Washington anyway. We chose . What failed on January 6, 2021 triumphed on January 20, 2025. It was all part of the same sustained, coordinated assault on our democratic form of government. After the initial setback, the MAGA armies have been steadily advancing. And now the house that is our structure of state is being razed. The Second American Revolution is drawing to its close. Democracy is on life support. The nation is under fascist occupation. And most of the country hasn't the foggiest notion that there's even a war on. Blue Amp 's a simple idea: Insiders turned fearless outsiders telling you what others won 't. We 're un-bought; We Need Your Support.Please become a paid subscriber The narrative of Trump ushering in another American Revolution is not my invention. The MAGA thought leaders have been invoking Revolutionary War language and peacocking in Revolutionary War imagery for a solid decade now. The blown-up posters of the Constitution and the Declaration of Independence, the "Don't Tread On Me" flags, the countless references to the Boston Tea Party, even the monomaniacal focus on tax reform--all of that is deliberate and of a piece. Rep. Lauren Boebert's notorious tweet on the morning of J6 is an example of this performative gesture: | | ---|---|--- Steve Bannon's podcast is called The War Room for a reason. "I'm not a journalist. I'm not in the media. This is a military headquarters for a populist revolt," . "You're a conservative, but you're not dangerous. You're reasonable. We're not reasonable. We're unreasonable because we're fighting for a republic. And we're never going to be reasonable until we get what we [want]. We're not looking to compromise. We're looking to win....[O]n the fundamental direction of the country, we are separate. We are two different worldviews. And those worldviews can't be bridged." The republic he's fighting for is a republic in the same way that a Soviet Socialist Republic was a republic--populist or not, he's no small-d democrat--but Bannon is right about the irreconcilable differences of the two factions. This is a binary choice. The country can't very well be a democracy and a dictatorship. Disgraced former national security adviser Mike Flynn has long couched his rightwing nationalist inklings in Revolutionary War rhetoric--part of an extensive psy-op to frame corruption, criminality, and treason as some sort of patriotic enterprise. Before the election, we were in a third American Revolution: | | ---|---|--- After the inauguration, Flynn discounted the Civil War and got down to two. Note his casual use of a Benjamin Franklin quote to dress sedition in Revolutionary War garb: | | ---|---|--- And you won't be surprised to learn that the Apartheid Nazi billionaire tasked with dismantling the so-called Administrative State--and plundering the nation's resources (money, data, technology, land, water, etc.) in the process, to create a Russia-in-the-90s-style oligarchy--has also taken to wrapping himself in the flag. (Also note: he stole the idea from someone else, as is his wont.) | | ---|---|--- | | ---|---|--- To the true believers, Donald isn't the leader of the movement as much as the movement's secret weapon--an instrument by which the presidency becomes a throne, and our nation's power and wealth are redistributed to the MAGA oligarchs. "Trump is a revolution," Bannon tells Brooks. "Remember, General Washington, the revolution and the foundation, and then Lincoln, the birth of the new America....I hate to say this, but Trump's the third. Trump is taking America back to its more constitutional Republic for the third time...." Google "Second American Revolution" and many of the responses ping back to remarks that Kevin Roberts-- head of the Heritage Foundation, and prime mover of Project 2025--. "In spite of all this nonsense from the Left, we are going to win," he assured the War Room listeners. "We're in the process of taking this country back. No one in the audience should be despairing....[W]e are in the process of the Second American Revolution, which will remain bloodless if the Left allows it to be." Whoa, Nelly! A Second American Revolution, which will remain bloodless if the Left allows it to be. That was a provocative thing for anyone to say, much less the head of the Heritage Foundation, an august conservative think tank founded over half a century ago; its executive director is not some fringe podcaster, out Nazi, or QAnon freak. In that interview, Roberts confirmed that he and his comrades were openly attempting to overthrow the government(that is, after all, what "revolution" means), and he doubled down with an implicit threat of mass violence. That the threat was veiled made it no less ominous. Distilling the theory Ted Gurr lays out in Why Men Rebel , Skocpol writes: "Political violence occurs when many people in society become angry, especially if existing cultural and practical conditions provide encouragement for aggression against political targets." Well, tens of millions of Americans are fucking furious right now--all across the political spectrum. Trump has spent years subtly encouraging political violence; the point of his calling out specific judges by name, for example, is to foment stochastic terrorism. Other MAGA leaders, like Mike Flynn's flying monkey and self-styled Secretary of Retribution Ivan Raiklin, have been more explicit. On the other side of the horseshoe, plenty of progressives have lionized Luigi Mangione. As for "practical conditions," I mean, this is a nation armed to the teeth; our citizenry is better armed than most armies. Given all the powder in the keg, an uptick in political violence seems inevitable--just a matter of time. Any political rhetoric that fans the flames could make the whole thing explode. By talking like that, Roberts was openly flirting with disaster. Even the bumbling legacy media seemed to appreciate that Roberts had crossed some sort of dangerous line. But it ended there. For the most part, the press failed to connect the dots between the Heritage Foundation, the other sponsors of Project 2025, Leonard Leo's dark-money operations, the Dark Enlightenment thought leaders (including the now-VP JD Vance), the libertarian arm of Silicon Valley, Flynn and his acolytes, the Bannon contingent, the Christian Nationalists, and various and sundry other groups united in common cause against "the Cathedral," "the Regime," "the Administrative State," and, ultimately, democracy. Trump's disavowal of Project 2025 on the campaign trail--an obvious lie even in the moment--was enough to get the legacy media hacks off the scent. But Roberts, to his credit, has always been perfectly transparent about the movement's intentions. I mean, they literally wrote a book about what they were planning to do! Two weeks ago, Roberts, , gave a sort of progress report of the Second American Revolution. After opening with a reference to Patrick Henry's "Liberty or Death" speech--"one of my personal favorites"--he writes: > The good news is that we are well on our way. Right now, President Trump's executive orders and decisive actions are systematically dismantling the deep state's hard power. On his first day in office, he terminated all remote work arrangements for federal employees. Then, he introduced an unprecedented, deferred resignation program which convinced more than 75,000 unelected bureaucrats to resign their posts. And last month he signed a new executive order requiring all federal agencies to fire at least four people for every one person they hire. Meanwhile, the Department of Government Efficiency's (DoGE) incredible work has undermined the regime's soft power by exposing its waste and corruption unlike ever before. > Make no mistake, these have only been the opening skirmishes in what will be a long fight. Though President Trump's has already delivered a major blow, the deep state remains powerful. Slowly but surely, it is forming its counteroffensive. Corrupt federal judges are blocking the President's executive orders. Colleges and universities are disguising their DEI programs to prevent losing federal funding. Countless bureaucrats are laying low in federal agencies, waiting for the opportunity to upend the people's agenda. Even as fewer and fewer people are tuning in, the mainstream media is still doing everything it can to disparage Trump.... > We are living in the Second American Revolution. We are stronger than we've ever been, and we must use every moral lever of power at our disposal to restore our founding principles, recover the basic norms of Western civilization (such as the notion that men and women are different), and revive the traditional institutions of American life by restoring their original purpose. > Despite the left's fearmongering, none of this requires violence. But it does require us to remember the promise of Patrick Henry that "millions of people, armed in the holy cause of liberty, and in such a country as that which we possess, are invincible by any force which our enemy can send against us." Roberts's characterization of the Trump/Musk actions as anything other than corrupt, oligarchical, petty, cruel, arbitrary, and profoundly antidemocratic--and therefore unequivocally unAmerican--is either irrefutable evidence of severe mental illness or willful and weaponized mendacity. In his writing, he is fond of inserting allusions to conservative heroes like Ronald Reagan (as in ) or, here, Patrick Henry, and then deliberately taking the quotes out of context to make it seem like these liberty-loving legends would somehow sign on to his destruction of our democracy. The irony is that the Second American Revolution seeks to resurrect the tyranny the first one stamped out. Seventeen seventy-six was Star Wars ; 2025 is The Empire Strikes Back. We need Luke to finish his training, Han Solo to thaw, and the Ewoks to get involved right quick, or we're looking at a bleak, regressive, Darth Vader future. Blue Amp 's a simple idea: Insiders turned fearless outsiders telling you what others won 't. We 're un-bought; We Need Your Support.Please become a paid subscriber I bring all this up not to be defeatist but to properly frame the struggle. We--that is: the media, Congress, Wall Street, and the American people generally--are in denial about the grim reality of the situation. Before we can possibly build a viable resistance movement, we have to face five key facts: 1\. The Second American Revolution is a war. "We are stronger than we've ever been, and we must use every moral lever of power at our disposal," Roberts , before assuring us that "none of this requires violence." That's a callback to his "bloodless" comment back in July. The implication is that the MAGA side is perfectly willing to take up arms and slaughter the rest of us if need be. Is there any reason to believe otherwise? We need to stop pretending that this is all a friendly disagreement between two democratic parties. It's not. Bannon is absolutely right when he says, "We are two different worldviews. And those worldviews can't be bridged." (I think a lot of Democrats realize this--it animated the seemingly disproportionate anger at the useless fool Chuck Schumer, who is hopelessly stuck in a past that no longer exists.) 2\. Every day Trump stays in power it becomes harder to dislodge him. Even if the House impeached him (which it won't as long as his compromised bootlicker Mike Johnson remains the Speaker) and the Senate convicted him (which it very well might if a vote was cast tomorrow), do we really think he'd voluntarily leave? Who would remove him from the premises? The Praetorian Guard that is the Secret Service? The DOGE goon squad the U.S Marshals have become? The Senate Sergeant at Arms? Pete Fucking Hegseth? Good luck with that. 3\. There is no viable constitutional remedy to correct course. To re-establish a functional democracy and reclaim our standing as the leaders of the free world, the entire MAGA cancer must be excised from the political body: Trump, Vance, Speaker Johnson, the entire Cabinet, every last one of his appointees and hires. All the demons, cast out. There is no template for how that would work; Madison and Jefferson didn't game-plan for wholesale treason. (Is there a way to compel all of the traitors to resign en masse? Maybe trading resignation for immunity?) By whatever method, they must all go, and then we must hold new elections as soon as possible--but what happens until President Ocasio-Cortez is sworn in? What does an interim government look like? Under what authority does it exist? 4\. Some of the damage is irreversible. Trillions of dollars in wealth have evaporated because of Trump's rock-dumb tariff obsession. Decades of careful humanitarian work went up in smoke when DOGE froze payments to USAID and then blew it up. Russia has been given a lifeline, more time to rally its troops, and fewer defenses to worry about in Ukraine. Bibi is running amok in Gaza. Scientific research hit a brick wall. A new pandemic would potentially wipe a lot of us out because of RFK cuts. Hundreds of thousands if not millions of American lives have been disrupted. Europe doesn't trust us anymore. And Canada now hates us so much that t. These are just a few examples; someone could write a long book about how much damage he and Musk have already done--and they're just getting started. This cacophony is merely the overture to a tedious, artless, interminable opera of stupid. 5\. The country cannot survive four years of this. We're a mere 78 days into the second Trump Administration. Assuming the Redux is a "normal" four-year term of 1461 days--and, spoiler alert, if Trump is still alive and in office in 2028, he is absolutely going to run for a third term, Twenty Second Amendment be damned--95 percent of his second term is still to come. What fresh horrors await us? Hyperinflation? Martial law imposed on April 20? Roundup of dissidents and free transport to El Salvador? A deadlier pandemic? Nuclear war? It's like heading into the finale of White Lotus : anything is possible, but the only thing we know for sure is that people we love are going to die. It is encouraging that Kevin Roberts says we are "living in the Second American Revolution" rather than gloating about his side's total victory. But while Revolution No. 2 may not be complete, it has already succeeded in its first task: destroying the Ancien R egime Americain (incidentally, one of the things the Unabomber goes on about in his manifesto that all these Dark Enlightenment dorks obsess over). We must take the "L" and waste no further time crying over the spilled milk of our former hegemony. To win--to have any hope of winning--we must move forward. The silver lining here is that if MAGA can be thwarted--if we can, in their parlance, take back America --that would give us the opportunity to repair the Constitutional fault lines that work against sustained democracy. On paper, this ain't hard: Get rid of the Electoral College. Figure out a way to make the Senate more representative of the nation as a whole (i.e., Wyoming should not have the same number of Senators as California). Expand the Supreme Court and/or impose term limits. Kill Citizens United like it's a puppy and we're Kristi Noem. Make D.C. and Puerto Rico states. That won't put together what Trump has permanently broken, but it would strengthen our democracy going forward. And here's a final irony. After ten years of Trump repeating what is, to him, a meaningless catchphrase, it will be left to us, the real patriots, the true custodians of this democracy, to make America great again. Let the Counterrevolution begin! Maybe a tiered system, where top 10 in population get 3 senators, bottom 10 get 1? * Greg Olear is the L.A. Times -bestselling author of three novels and four nonfiction books, including (2024). His newest book is . He cohosts the weekly live show and hosts the PREVAIL podcast. His Substack column, , has run since 2019. | A guest post by| novelist, #RoughBeast & #DirtyRubles author, PREVAIL columnist & podcaster, co-host of The Five 8, enjoyer of Friday Manhattans. name: Jazz Lewis
subject: April 2025 Newsletter: Post-Session Review
body:
https://www.jazzlewis.com/ [https://www.jazzlewis.com/]
April 2025 Newsletter: Post-Session Review
Budget & Fiscal Leadership
Despite starting the session with a $3.3 billion deficit, exacerbated by federal disruptions and mass layoffs, Maryland passed a balanced, forward-thinking budget that protects our core values.
As House Majority Whip and a senior member of the Appropriations Committee, I helped lead efforts to preserve our Rainy Day Fund, protect services like Medicaid and developmental disability programs, and fund future-focused investments in education, infrastructure, and small business growth. We cut more than $2 billion in spending and raised $1.5 billion in new revenue, allowing us to maintain a structural surplus while continuing to fully fund the Blueprint for Maryland’s Future.
Our budget boosts direct school aid by over 7%, expands quantum computing at the University of Maryland, invests in workforce development, and supports local law enforcement and victims of crime. Even in tough times, we chose people over politics—and made sure no Marylander was left behind.
Legislation Passed
This session, I introduced and passed two major bills:
HB43 – Federally Qualified Health Centers Grant Program [https://mgaleg.maryland.gov/mgawebsite/Legislation/Details/hb0043#:~:text=Authorizing%20the%20Board%20of%20Public,a%20federally%20qualified%20health%20center.]
Allows state grants for land acquisition and expansion of health centers that serve all Marylanders, regardless of income, expanding care to underserved communities.
HB1424 – Protect Our Federal Workers Act [https://mgaleg.maryland.gov/mgawebsite/Legislation/Details/hb1424]
Provides interest-free loans and enhanced unemployment support for laid-off federal workers, empowers the Attorney General to take legal action, and streamlines hiring into state jobs. Maryland stands with its federal workforce.
Additionally, I advocated and backed numerous other bills that will enable Marylanders to have the quality of life we deserve. Check out some of them below:
Education
I supported HB504, strengthening oversight of the Blueprint for Maryland’s Future while allowing flexibility in implementation. The results: more full-day Pre-K, improved literacy scores, and expanded community schools.
Housing
I backed several bills to protect tenants and expand affordable housing:
* HB127: Nonprofits can help administer housing rehab programs
* HB390: Allows tax breaks for long-term affordable housing
* HB585: Requires landlords to share contact info
* HB767: Extends eviction notice periods and tenant protections
Healthcare
We passed key bills to keep healthcare affordable and accessible:
* HB718: Monitors federal changes to protect state healthcare
* HB974: Guarantees no-cost preventive services
* HB1045 & HB1082: Boosts consumer protections and maintains subsidies
* HB297 & HB869: Lowers premiums and expands telehealth access
Energy
I supported HB1035–1037, a transformative energy package delivering $200M in ratepayer relief, ending unfair practices, and boosting clean energy projects. We’re investing in affordability, innovation, and environmental protection.
Prince George’s County Investments
I continue to make it a top priority to deliver meaningful investments to our community here in the 24th district. As Chair of the Public Safety and Administration Subcommittee and Vice-Chair of the Capital Budget Subcommittee, I secured critical resources for Prince George’s County.
Operating Budget Highlights:
* Prince George’s County Public Schools: $1.7 billion
* Prince George’s Community College: $52.8 million
* Public Libraries: $8.8 million
* Public Safety: $22.6 million
* Fire & EMS: $1.9 million
* Health Grants: $8.3 million
Capital Budget Wins:
* $20M for obstetric care at Doctor’s Community Hospital
* $10.9M for Zupnik Hall at University of Maryland
* $10M for MedStar Southern Maryland Hospital
* $5M for Fort Washington Adventist Health Hospital
* $3M for mixed-income housing near Capitol Heights Metro
* $2.9M for Greater Baden Medical Services
* $2.2M for Thurgood Marshall Library at Bowie State
Plus, community upgrades for Rosecroft Raceway, local playgrounds, Boys and Girls Clubs, Goodwill, and more.
Be sure to follow me on social media to stay updated:
Facebook @JazzLewis
Instagram: @jazzlewisformaryland
Twitter: @JazzforMaryland
Bluesky: @jazzformaryland.bsky.social
https://www.jazzlewis.com/ [https://www.jazzlewis.com/]
Delegate Jazz Lewis
Democrat, District 24, Prince George's County
Taylor House Office Building, Room 151
6 Bladen St., Annapolis, MD 21401
800-492-7122, ext. 3691; jazz.lewis@house.state.md.us
www.jazzlewis.com
By Authority: Friends of Jazz Lewis, Chris Taitt, Treasurer
Friends of Jazz Lewis - State Account
P.O. Box 341854
Bethesda, MD 20827
United States

The U.S. Military Academy and the U.S. Air Force Academy will no
longer consider race as a factor in admissions as the military schools
had long done to boost enrollment of Black, Hispanic and other
minorities, President Donald Trump's administration said. The
changes were detailed in court filings by the Department of Justice
in federal courts in two lawsuits filed by a group that successfully
persuaded the U.S. Supreme Court to ban race-conscious admissions at
civilian universities.

clean_body: Background on the end of DEI in military academy admissions [NEWSLINK] BREAKING NEWS... [image] [] RIGHTS AND FREEDOMS IN PERIL "When it comes to fighting for the American people’s ‘right to know,’ no one holds a candle to Tom Fitton and his team at Judicial Watch" - SEAN HANNITY Tom Fitton returns with an exhaustive investigation into the progressive movement’s efforts to dismantle the venerable institutions of American rights and freedoms. Order Tom Fitton's Must-Read Today! [] [600x300] [] US military academies end race consideration in admissions [] Source: Reuters [] The U.S. Military Academy and the U.S. Air Force Academy will no longer consider race as a factor in admissions as the military schools had long done to boost enrollment of Black, Hispanic and other minorities, President Donald Trump's administration said. The changes were detailed in court filings by the Department of Justice in federal courts in two lawsuits filed by a group that successfully persuaded the U.S. Supreme Court to ban race-conscious admissions at civilian universities. [13x13x1] [] READ MORE [] Background Notes from Judicial Watch [180x150x1] [] October 09, 2024 Judicial Watch Sues Department of Defense for Records on U.S. Air Force Academy’s Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion Plans [] Source: Judicial Watch [] Judicial Watch filed a FOIA lawsuit on behalf of Stand Together Against Racism and Radicalism in the Services, Inc. (STARRS) against the Department of Defense for records regarding the U.S. Air Force Academy’s diversity, equity, and inclusion (DEI) plans. [13x13x1] [] READ MORE [] [180x150x2] [] February 20, 2025 Hegseth Deserves a Medal for Stopping DEI in the Military [] Source: Judicial Watch [] Judicial Watch President TomFitton discusses Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth’s role in stopping DEI in our military. [13x13x1] [] READ MORE [] [180x150x3] [] November 13, 2024 Judicial Watch Sues for Records on Rebranding of West Point’s DEI Office [] Source: Judicial Watch [] Judicial Watch sued the Department of Defense for information regarding the rebranding of West Point’s Diversity, Equity and Inclusion (DEI) office to “Office of Engagement and Retention.” “It seems games are afoot at West Point to disguise its radical DEI agenda,” said Judicial Watch President Tom Fitton. name: Justice Democrats
subject: Trump doubled down on deporting U.S. citizens
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY JUSTICE DEMOCRATS
body:
[1]Justice Democrats

Peter,

The Trump administration admitted they made an “administrative error” when
they kidnapped and deported Kilmar Abrego Garcia — a Salvadoran immigrant
and father from Maryland with a court order protecting him from
deportation — to El Salvador’s notorious mega-prison with more than 200
other immigrants who were not given due process.

Then, the U.S. Supreme Court UNANIMOUSLY ruled that Trump must facilitate
Kilmar’s return to the United States. Even right-wing extremists Clarence
Thomas and Samuel Alito couldn’t defend this.

Yesterday, Trump and El Salvador’s dictator met in the Oval Office where
both claimed they couldn’t do anything to free Kilmar and bring him home —
while Trump doubled down on his desire to send U.S. citizens to the same
foreign mega-prison.

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/signup/em-20250415-trump-el-salvador/?source=em20250415-36588&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&akid=36588%2E1203867%2EyjzeOo ]Tweet from The Bulwark quoting text from a video of the meeting between
President Trump and the President of El Salvador. " Q:" You mentioned that
you're open to deporting individuals that aren't foreign aliens...Does
that include U.S. citizens?" Trump: "If they're criminals...Yeah, that
includes them...I'm all for it." "

We have a White House allowing foreign leaders to refuse to abide by
Supreme Court rulings live from the Oval Office, while the President
himself is soft-launching deporting U.S. citizens to be tortured in El
Salvador.

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/signup/em-20250415-trump-el-salvador/?source=em20250415-36588&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&akid=36588%2E1203867%2EyjzeOo ]This is a constitutional crisis if we’ve ever seen one,
Peter, and we need to fight back NOW. Add your
name to demand Democrats and the GOP in Congress hold Trump accountable
for its fascist actions and return Kilmar Abrego Garcia to the U.S. NOW!

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/signup/em-20250415-trump-el-salvador/?source=em20250415-36588&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&akid=36588%2E1203867%2EyjzeOo ]Add Your Name

Democrats must fight back with every tool they have — not just on MSNBC —
and the Republican Party that enables this blatant fascism should not
survive to see another cycle.

In solidarity,

Justice Democrats

Contribute to Justice Democrats: https://secure.actblue.com/donate/justicedemocrats?refcode=em20250415-36588-footer&t=1002&refcode2=36588_1203867_yjzeOo&akid=36588%2E1203867%2EyjzeOo

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way for us to reach you about opportunities to get involved. If you need to remove yourself from our email list, click here to unsubscribe: https://go.justicedemocrats.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=1003&akid=36588%2E1203867%2EyjzeOo

Lancaster PA 17603

subject: Elon Musk almost bought this race

## Nevada Removes Over 162,000 Names from Voter Rolls
THE CENTER SQUARE—The Nevada Secretary of State’s Office announced Monday that it had removed 162,519 voters from its voter registrations. Furthermore, the state also deactivated 37,749 voters. In total, the state removed 200,268 people from being able to vote. “The Secretary of State’s Office takes the transparency, security and accessibility of our elections very seriously, and is [...] [Read more](http://links.conservativeum.com/s/c/WqqDY1N6zu8600KJybRBn4-e2HRswOg31USUnGjiDJJ8lBPo_OMCKa1UBZW_AaE3VXxz_S_KRJnhqpVjtE4Qp29TXIFt_XJYDSHVTtNqj-JAKTeadG1jREw40gKW0ZqEULdBKSaSEue3EeFrqjYpd8V81mU_tOcPF-QcpijIEcwrMpzXsLc-L1HkyIV0S9AxQuWgFPLqCOKwpus-tGyaOeX9LvjuYLyVmH7FJ8YqrXGgKy91IA53_N9fZ_xVnbVcljr_DWdm9-162NnR_HDVvQ-q5bxDVnlVBwFHAq9nPflfeRcKq3ECtROeYhiQ71q0-YRd1KzL_SFpMA/N5wOwTficghCPsO7UvZ7sOOhhYzRRkDK/8)

## ‘So Fake, Cringe’ – RedState
Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez drew criticism on social media after debuting a new "fake" accent while slandering President Trump as a "rapist" and criminal. AOC (D-NY) was speaking at a "Fighting Oligarchy" rally alongside Sen. Bernie Sanders (I-VT). ## Jury Finds Illegal Alien Guilty in Rachel Morin Murder, Rape Case
A Maryland jury has found an illegal alien guilty of murdering Rachel Morin, a mother of five. Victor Martinez-Hernandez was charged with first-degree murder, first-degree rape, third-degree sexual offense, and kidnapping in Morin’s case. The jury found Martinez-Hernandez guilty on all four counts. name: Team Deb Haaland
subject: A Republican challenger just entered the race!
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR AND AUTHORIZED BY DEB FOR NEW MEXICO
body:
There is only ONE campaign with the grassroots power to win this election.
# [](https://act.debhaaland.com/go/133?t=1&akid=1353%2E400544%2Epric5i)
**_BREAKING NEWS:_**
**A Republican challenger just entered the race for Governor of New Mexico!**
The stakes just got higher — but we knew this day would come, and we’re ready.
Here’s the plan: _Scare them with the strength of the grassroots movement behind Deb Haaland’s campaign._
**That’s why we’re setting an emergency fundraising goal to raise $100,000 in the next 48 hours.**
**[Can Deb count on you to pitch in $5 today to help us reach this critically important goal?](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_dh_20250415_1_opponent?amount=5&refcode=em20250415-1353&refcode2=1353_400544_pric5i&akid=1353%2E400544%2Epric5i)**
**[Pitch in now »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_dh_20250415_1_opponent?amount=5&refcode=em20250415-1353&refcode2=1353_400544_pric5i&akid=1353%2E400544%2Epric5i)**
This isn’t just about dollars. It’s about proving that we’re the campaign with the momentum to win this thing. That we’re the campaign fighting for real solutions. That we have Deb’s back.
Because the truth is there’s only **ONE** candidate in the race with real solutions to our struggles.
There’s only **ONE** campaign that has crisscrossed New Mexico to meet voters in 19 communities.
And there is only **ONE** campaign with the grassroots power to win this election. But we need your help to do it.
**[Can you help us get it done? Even one dollar makes a big difference when we all chip in:](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_dh_20250415_1_opponent?amount=5&refcode=em20250415-1353&refcode2=1353_400544_pric5i&akid=1353%2E400544%2Epric5i)**
**[Donate $5 »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_dh_20250415_1_opponent?amount=5&refcode=em20250415-1353&refcode2=1353_400544_pric5i&akid=1353%2E400544%2Epric5i)**

name: Wendy with Team Kaptur
subject: An onslaught
body:
Friend, an onslaught of dark money attacks are coming our way. We must be prepared to defend one of the most important Blue seats in Congress.

| |
---
Friend, an onslaught of dark money attacks are coming our way. Elon Musk has shown he's willing to spend tens of millions of dollars to turn our democracy into an oligarchy — and our district will once again be a top target in 2026. **UPDATE: North Carolina Supreme Court ruling could jeopardize legally cast ballots of overseas voters and military members**
Peter:
Did you see the news about the North Carolina Supreme Court’s ruling on Friday?
It’s the latest development in a case where anti-democracy extremists are working to overturn last November’s state supreme court election, which Justice Allison Riggs won.
For clearly partisan purposes, the losing candidate asked the court to disenfranchise overseas and military voters by cherrypicking which legally cast ballots are counted — sending a shameful message that their votes are questionable – and sadly, the court granted many of his obscene requests.
A.G. Holder’s response called it out loud and clear: **“This shocking decision abandons the judiciary’s most basic role, to protect the rights of the people, and sanctions an outright attempt to steal an election.”**
**In the face of attacks like this, we must keep fighting for our democracy. At the All On The Line campaign, we’ll continue mobilizing grassroots activists in North Carolina and across the nation to fight for fair elections and fair maps.[Make a donation and help power the movement for a truly representative democracy.](https://click.everyaction.com/k/105894939/539737960/-877614143?refcode=don_aotl_tier1_email_NCRR_email_20250415-AOTL-Tier1-NCRR_&utm_source=email&utm_medium=email&utm_term=don&utm_content=20250415-AOTL-Tier1-NCRR_&utm_campaign=NCRR&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxMy8xLzg5Mjk2IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImE5YmI2MzliLTM1MWEtZjAxMS04YjNkLTAwMjI0ODJhOWZiNyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=INNLkW2jtDwp3u85TfQhrh8tDIq4bW5WTCjtRfwZAow=)**

Now, how did we get to this ruling? After Justice Allison Riggs won re-election in November, her opponent, Jefferson Griffin, challenged more than 65,000 legally valid ballots, in an attempt to overturn the election results.
As a result, the North Carolina Supreme Court blocked the certification of Justice Riggs’ victory.
And now the court’s majority is jeopardizing legally cast ballots from military and overseas voters.
It’s clear that partisan extremists are using every tool at their disposal to try to steal this election. If left unchecked, they will try to replicate this playbook nationwide.
**As Justice Riggs continues her court fight to uphold the will of the people, All On the Line will keep fighting to protect our democracy. And we count on grassroots support to help power this work.**

clean_body: Have you seen the latest? | UPDATE: North Carolina Supreme Court ruling could jeopardize legally cast ballots of overseas voters and military members Peter: Did you see the news about the North Carolina Supreme Court’s ruling on Friday? It’s the latest development in a case where anti-democracy extremists are working to overturn last November’s state supreme court election, which Justice Allison Riggs won. For clearly partisan purposes, the losing candidate asked the court to disenfranchise overseas and military voters by cherrypicking which legally cast ballots are counted — sending a shameful message that their votes are questionable – and sadly, the court granted many of his obscene requests. A.G. Holder’s response called it out loud and clear: “This shocking decision abandons the judiciary’s most basic role, to protect the rights of the people, and sanctions an outright attempt to steal an election.” In the face of attacks like this, we must keep fighting for our democracy. At the All On The Line campaign, we’ll continue mobilizing grassroots activists in North Carolina and across the nation to fight for fair elections and fair maps.= If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: = | = = | = = | = Now, how did we get to this ruling? After Justice Allison Riggs won re-election in November, her opponent, Jefferson Griffin, challenged more than 65,000 legally valid ballots, in an attempt to overturn the election results. As a result, the North Carolina Supreme Court blocked the certification of Justice Riggs’ victory. And now the court’s majority is jeopardizing legally cast ballots from military and overseas voters. It’s clear that partisan extremists are using every tool at their disposal to try to steal this election. If left unchecked, they will try to replicate this playbook nationwide. As Justice Riggs continues her court fight to uphold the will of the people, All On the Line will keep fighting to protect our democracy. And we count on grassroots support to help power this work. = Thank you, The All On The Line Team is the grassroots advocacy campaign supported by the National Redistricting Action Fund. Support our work to end gerrymandering. Text FAIR to 88228 to receive recurring updates from AOTL by automated text message. Text HELP for help, STOP to end. name: VoteVets
subject: Trump’s attack on the Constitution
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY VOTEVETS, VOTEVETS
body:
On the campaign trail, Trump vowed to be his supporters “retribution.” He threatened to “root out the vermin.” After becoming president, he weaponized the Department of Justice and signed executive orders directing them to pursue critics and political opponents.
#
Peter:
A legal resident of the United States was deported to El Salvador early in Trump’s administration. He was married to a U.S. citizen, his children were U.S. citizens, and he had a work permit.
A "Withholding of Removal" order was issued in 2019 preventing his deportation. A federal court ordered Donald Trump not to deport him while his deportation flight was in progress. Trump’s administration admitted he was deported in error. After his deportation, a federal court ordered that he be returned. And now, after appeal, the Supreme Court has ordered Donald Trump to return him to the United States.
Kilmar Abrego Garcia is now allegedly in a prison in El Salvador alongside hundreds of others deported from the United States without charges or due process. And when the president of El Salvador visited Trump in the White House yesterday, they both refused to facilitate his return.
Trump is refusing to follow an order from the Supreme Court. But somehow, it gets worse than that.
At the meeting, Trump said: **"Home-growns are next. The home-growns. You gotta build about five more places. It’s not big enough."** It used to be that you had to take Trump seriously, but not literally. Over the last four months we have all learned that’s no longer the case.
On the campaign trail, Trump vowed to be his supporters “retribution.” He threatened to “root out the vermin.” After becoming president, he weaponized the Department of Justice and signed executive orders directing them to pursue critics and political opponents. And now, he wants to send American citizens to foreign prisons.
The seriousness of this moment cannot be understated. VoteVets is an organization by Veterans, for Veterans. We advocate for Veterans, we support them, and we help them affect change as public servants. That remains our mission.
But our Constitutional order is being torn apart. We’ve each sworn an oath to support and defend the Constitution — and we will uphold that oath. Each of us must do what we can to defend our democracy now.
We’ve got to be ready to fight like never before. That’s what this moment demands of us, and we have to meet the call.
**VoteVets will not back down,** and we are going to continue defending our democracy. [**If you’re with us, and you’d like to support our mission chip in $5 or whatever you can today.**](https://act.votevets.org/go/2927?c0=vv-pac-em-april25.G9aCQU&refcode=em250415-1-120&t=1&akid=34349%2E3013755%2E6jGuAx)

name: Jasmine Crockett
subject: The Constitution isn’t optional, Donald
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Serve America PAC
body:
|
---
It’s simple: **The Constitution isn’t optional.** Yet rather than living in reality, Donald Trump seems to think he’s still on a reality show where the law doesn’t apply to him: [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/105895183/539738920/-225299136?refcode=250415_Serve_Crockett_NotOptional&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjUvMS83NDc1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4MWYwNTcyYi0zNjFhLWYwMTEtOGIzZC0wMDIyNDgyYTlmYjciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Met7og4j0nJs8ClUh7Bf4lRuCJsfCu93bhS8JKHgMgo=) This isn’t partisan — it’s just the law. And every single member of Congress, including Republicans, took an oath to uphold the Constitution when we were sworn into office. Yet I know that none of them will have the courage to stand up and call out this attack on our Constitution and the rule of law. That’s why Republicans can’t be trusted with power, and why I’m working overtime to put Democrats back in power. [**Can I count on you to join me in that fight by chipping in $10, $25, or more right now -- split with Serve America? Let’s put people who respect the law and our democracy back in power before it’s too late. >>**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/105895184/539738921/-225299136?refcode=250415_Serve_Crockett_NotOptional&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjUvMS83NDc1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4MWYwNTcyYi0zNjFhLWYwMTEtOGIzZC0wMDIyNDgyYTlmYjciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Met7og4j0nJs8ClUh7Bf4lRuCJsfCu93bhS8JKHgMgo=) [DONATE](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/105895185/539738922/-2068302509?refcode=250415_Serve_Crockett_NotOptional&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjUvMS83NDc1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4MWYwNTcyYi0zNjFhLWYwMTEtOGIzZC0wMDIyNDgyYTlmYjciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Met7og4j0nJs8ClUh7Bf4lRuCJsfCu93bhS8JKHgMgo=) Thank you,
Jasmine [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/105895186/539738923/-2068302509?refcode=250415_Serve_Crockett_NotOptional&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjUvMS83NDc1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4MWYwNTcyYi0zNjFhLWYwMTEtOGIzZC0wMDIyNDgyYTlmYjciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Met7og4j0nJs8ClUh7Bf4lRuCJsfCu93bhS8JKHgMgo=) [DONATE](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/105895187/539738924/-1451023484?refcode=250415_Serve_Crockett_NotOptional&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjUvMS83NDc1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4MWYwNTcyYi0zNjFhLWYwMTEtOGIzZC0wMDIyNDgyYTlmYjciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Met7og4j0nJs8ClUh7Bf4lRuCJsfCu93bhS8JKHgMgo=)

name: State Representative Ryan Chamberlin
subject: Newsletter from Rep. Ryan Chamberlin
body:
Newsletter from Rep. Ryan Chamberlin | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/repryanchamberlin/newsletter-from-rep-ryan-chamberlin-12927509?e=d1c1b9864f)
---
| |
---
| | **NEWSLETTER FROM REP. RYAN CHAMBERLIN**
---
| Friends and Neighbors,

Although the legislative session is beginning to wind down, we still have a lot to accomplish for our state in our remaining weeks in Tallahassee. It has been an honor to be the voice of our community this session and fight for the issues important to you, my constituents. I’m so proud of all that we’ve achieved and look forward to the positive impact these policies will have on our state in the years to come. Together, let’s keep our state the Free State of Florida and a shining beacon to the rest of the nation.

**I look forward to keeping you updated on our progress. Stay tuned!**
---
| As always, my office is here to serve you! Please do not hesitate to [**contact my team**](https://ryanchamberlin.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=ec15c6093052c02efbd22b6cd&id=6af408d5c6&e=d1c1b9864f) if you need assistance with anything. We would be happy to help in any way we can!
---
|
---
| | **Committee Meetings: Week 6**
---
| Along with being in session and meeting with constituents and stakeholders, this past week was busy with committee meetings. Click below to read the meeting packets from each committee meeting to learn more about the bills we heard.

**Week 6 Committee Meetings:**
* [Ways and Means Committee](https://ryanchamberlin.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=ec15c6093052c02efbd22b6cd&id=9865991310&e=d1c1b9864f)
* [Intergovernmental Affairs Subcommittee](https://ryanchamberlin.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=ec15c6093052c02efbd22b6cd&id=25c292a6e8&e=d1c1b9864f)

---
| | **Week 6: Update on My Legislation**
---
|
---
---
| I’m pleased to share that my bill, [CS/HB 4067 - Equestrian Sport Facilities in Marion County](https://ryanchamberlin.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=ec15c6093052c02efbd22b6cd&id=0f03e04549&e=d1c1b9864f), passed favorably in the Industries & Professional Activities Subcommittee. Only one stop left, and then it heads to the House Floor!

Another one of my bills, [CS/CS/HB 541 - Minimum Wage Requirements](https://ryanchamberlin.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=ec15c6093052c02efbd22b6cd&id=eb5b6e401c&e=d1c1b9864f), passed its final committee stop in the Commerce Committee. Now, on to the House Floor!
---
| | **Meetings in Tallahassee**
---
|
---
| The **Community Foundation for Ocala/Marion County** and 20 nonprofits from Ocala were able to squeeze into my office to talk about the process of appropriations and how the legislature works to help Florida’s nonprofits. I was grateful for the opportunity to meet with them!
---
|
---
| All of the **Marion County Commissioners** came up to Tallahassee for **Marion County Day** and stopped by my office to discuss their legislative priorities and bills that may affect our county. We had a productive and meaningful discussion!
---
|
---
| Representatives from **Cox Communications** swung by for a short meeting to talk about the success they are having in our community. I greatly appreciate all that they do for Marion County!
---
|
---
| While celebrating **Marion County Day** at the Capitol, we had a meeting with **AdventHealth Ocala**. They talked about the great programs they have and their efforts to provide better healthcare access to the Marion County area.
---
| | **Marion County Day at the Florida Capitol**
---
|
---
|
---
---
| The evening before we celebrated **Marion County Day** at the Capitol, the **Community Foundation for Ocala/Marion County** held a reception at the Governors Club to honor former **State Senator Dennis Baxley**. He did so much for our county while serving in the legislature, and we are truly grateful for all that he accomplished for our community. Following the reception, Marion County held a legislative dinner. It was a great opportunity to connect and enjoy a good time with the amazing people from our county.

On Thursday, the Capitol courtyard was overflowing with Marion County residents! We had **Big Lee’s world-famous BBQ** , **Marion County Sheriff’s Office Mounted Unit** , and various tents filled with education on how great our county truly is.
---
| | **Receptions & Events in Tallahassee**
---
| My staff and I attended various receptions and events in Tallahassee last week, including:
* **Miami Dade Days**
* **UCF Day “Luncheon”**
* **FAMU Day at the Capitol**
* **Tallahassee State College Reception**

---
|
---
| | Thank you for reading, and I look forward to sharing with you again soon.

Have a great week!

Best regards,

**Ryan Chamberlin**
******State Representative**
**District 24**
---
| | _Copyright © 2024 Ryan Chamberlin for State Representative, All rights reserved._

Paid by Ryan Chamberlin, Republican, for State Representative

**Our mailing address is:**
Ryan Chamberlin for State Representative
526 E Park Ave
Tallahassee, FL 32301-2568

Want to change how you receive these emails?
You can [update your preferences](https://ryanchamberlin.us21.list-manage.com/profile?u=ec15c6093052c02efbd22b6cd&id=929c76fdea&e=d1c1b9864f&c=d0ac88352d) or [unsubscribe from this list](https://ryanchamberlin.us21.list-manage.com/unsubscribe?u=ec15c6093052c02efbd22b6cd&id=929c76fdea&t=b&e=d1c1b9864f&c=d0ac88352d).

---

subject: what gives me hope

body:
I want to share with you five things that give me hope in this moment:

[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/105895287/539739314/-1080040549?refcode=email-header&&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85Mzg4OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0MTU3YWI4ZS0zNzFhLWYwMTEtOGIzZC0wMDIyNDgyYTlmYjciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=IEkLe6LZ1kjaUkC1itLWKUxPHy2iiD6QvwuvPqwHCwA=) Friend, I’ve heard from so many folks in our community that, right now, the state of our politics just feels hopeless. **I want to let you know that I hear you.** There’s so much cause for all of us to feel anger, outrage, and exhaustion. But one thing none of us should feel is hopeless, team, because I certainly don’t. If you have a moment, I’d like to share with you five things that give _me_ hope in this moment we’ve found ourselves in:
1. **The resilience of the American spirit.** As a nation, we’ve faced down greater challenges and come out on top. And I know we won’t give in now: _Americans don’t back down from a tough fight._

2. **Democratic State Attorneys General across the country** who are leading the charge to stop Trump and Musk’s illegal mass firings, prevent DOGE from accessing Americans’ private data, and restore education funding appropriated by Congress.

3. **Wisconsin’s Supreme Court race** proved that when we stand together, we can defeat right-wing billionaires who want to buy our elections, and instead choose Democracy.

4. **Americans are standing up for their rights in town halls across the nation,** even in ruby red districts where their GOP representatives have decided to hide rather than face their own voters.

5. **And finally, every upcoming election between now and 2026** is a new chance for Democrats to present our case to the American people and build a movement to ultimately reject MAGA extremism for good. We’re just getting started, and there’s still a long road ahead of us.

Sadly, Trump’s constant attacks on our democracy and the fundamental rule of law do keep me awake at night. **But folks, I refuse to give up.** I’m rallying supporters across OR-06 to fight back against Donald Trump’s agenda. I’m speaking out everywhere I can to condemn Republicans’ plan to rip away healthcare from millions of hardworking Americans. I’ve even called on House Republicans to join with Democrats and block Trump’s destructive tariffs. **[I can’t promise that we’ll win every fight. But I know that when we stand together, we can make a real difference. So I have to ask: Will you chip in $5 to stand with me as I fight back against Trump’s dangerous agenda?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/105895288/539739315/1012014768?amount=5&refcode=email_041525_hope&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85Mzg4OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0MTU3YWI4ZS0zNzFhLWYwMTEtOGIzZC0wMDIyNDgyYTlmYjciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=IEkLe6LZ1kjaUkC1itLWKUxPHy2iiD6QvwuvPqwHCwA=)** _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_ | | | | [Donate $5 →](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/105895289/539739316/1012014768?amount=5&express_lane=true&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500,1000&refcode=email_041525_hope&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85Mzg4OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0MTU3YWI4ZS0zNzFhLWYwMTEtOGIzZC0wMDIyNDgyYTlmYjciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=IEkLe6LZ1kjaUkC1itLWKUxPHy2iiD6QvwuvPqwHCwA=)
